summary: Threat-model walk of callback wire surface (Stages 1-4). One Medium and three Lows; zero High. Bounded by absence of HTTP/channel ingest of callbacks today; the Medium becomes High when that wiring lands. Auditor F1 (PositionWire bounds) and F2 (AskCallback !-prefix filter) re-verified closed.

findings.title: callback.run skips signing.verify when RawSignature is null
findings.summary: PlangDataSerializer.FromEnvelope sets Data.Signature directly from the envelope JSON; an attacker omitting the signature field results in RawSignature==null, callback.run skips signing.verify entirely, and dispatch proceeds with attacker-chosen Position + Variables. v1 = identity *encryption* doesn't excuse this — *signing* is real (Ed25519). Bounded today by lack of channel ingest; will become exploitable as soon as Stage 5 / HTTP wiring lands callback receive.
findings.fix_recommendation: Auto-EnsureSigned for in-process ICallback dispatch (symmetric posture wire vs in-process), then make callback.run reject RawSignature==null. Three workable shapes detailed in summary.md.
findings.exploitability_today: narrow — only in-process, where the trust boundary is fine

findings.title: Variables.Restore + ErrorCallback wire path miss !-prefix filter
findings.summary: Variables.Capture filters !-prefix names (system/infra: !app, !ask.answer, !error.*). Variables.Restore does not. ErrorCallback's wire path goes through Restore and lets attacker-supplied !-prefixed names through. Mirror of auditor F2 closure (which fixed only AskCallback's bind loop in 3fbc7cfa); the fix didn't propagate to the source-of-truth path or the ErrorCallback variant. Compounds with F1 — unsigned ErrorCallback can inject !ask.answer to short-circuit a downstream ask.
findings.fix_recommendation: Add `if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(data.Name) && data.Name.StartsWith("!")) continue;` to Variables.@this.Restore. One-line fix; covers ErrorCallback wire and any future Snapshot consumer.

findings.title: Callback wire JSON deserialize has no body size / list cap
findings.summary: JsonSerializer.Deserialize on callback wires uses default options — no MaxBytes, no Variables-list cap. crypto.decrypt is identity (v1), so the buffer arrives unchanged. Data.@this's GZipDecompress already caps at 100MB but callback wire bypasses gzip. Multi-GB body fed into JsonSerializer.Deserialize OOMs the parser.
findings.fix_recommendation: Add MaxBytes / MaxVariables constants and reject oversized wires. Mirrors http module's existing size-limits pattern.
findings.blocking: before-channel-wiring (channel-layer bound is the primary control; this is defense-in-depth)

findings.title: Callback wire serializers don't apply SensitivePropertyFilter
findings.summary: Data.@this._envelopeJsonOptions applies SensitivePropertyFilter.Strip; the three callback-wire option blocks above do not. Variables captured into a callback include the full Data envelope including [Sensitive]-marked Value sub-properties, which then cross the wire un-redacted. Today only Identity.PrivateKey is [Sensitive] and rarely lands in user-namespace variables, so practical exposure is narrow — but the architectural pattern teaches the wrong thing as more types get marked.
findings.fix_recommendation: Add `Modifiers = { SensitivePropertyFilter.Strip }` to each of the three option blocks. One line each. Mirrors `Data.@this._envelopeJsonOptions`.

notes.title: CallbackGoal* errors propagate as CLR exceptions
notes.summary: Auditor's N3. Combined with F1, an attacker satisfying the signature gate but sending a bad position throws an unhandled CLR exception out of cb.Run. Caller framing decides whether this surfaces as 500 vs controlled error. Worth threading through Data.FromError when channel wiring lands.

notes.title: Providers.@this.Restore Assembly.LoadFrom path is wire-unreachable in current shape
notes.summary: Providers.Restore does Assembly.LoadFrom(reg.Source); reg.Source comes from the Snapshot. Neither AskCallback nor ErrorCallback wires populate the Providers section (only CallStack + Variables). So the wire cannot reach Assembly.LoadFrom today. Standing rule (memory: small-rules in MEMORY.md) already covers Module.add/provider.load as accepted-trust on .pr. **If** ErrorCallback's wire format is ever extended to round-trip Providers, this becomes Critical — pre-emptive note for the architect.

notes.title: ActorName, ActionModule, ActionName, Id are wire fields with no consumer
notes.summary: Wire shapes carry these fields but Restore doesn't read them. Inert today. Worth dropping to shrink attack surface and wire size.

F1_PositionWire_bounds.comment: Both stepIndex and actionIndex bounds guards present, mirror CallStack.this.Snapshot.cs:131-136. CallbackGoalNotFound thrown with descriptive context.

F2_AskCallback_bang_prefix_filter.comment: AskCallback.Run bind loop now skips !-prefix names. *But* the same fix is missing on Variables.@this.Restore (the path ErrorCallback wires use) — see F2 above.

csharp_tunit.rebuild: clean (rm -rf bin obj for PlangConsole/PLang/PLang.Tests/PLang.Generators, then dotnet build PlangConsole)

plang.result: 192 [Pass] entries; 6 [Fail] are intentional fixture goals under _fixtures_sensitive/ and _fixtures_fail/ consumed by meta-tests (matches tester v1 accounting)

recommendation: merge after F1 fix scheduled before any channel-side wiring of callback ingest; bundle F2 + F4 (one-liners) into the same pass; F3 fine to defer to channel wiring with channel-layer bound as primary control.
